methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis considers a peer universe of 11 comparable companies in Biotech & Health—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

A single applicable method anchors this analysis. Stage-progression valuation applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $26M total raised as a proxy for current value, yielding an implied valuation of $65M. This multiplier reflects typical step-up dynamics from seed to the subsequent financing round in biotech. Peer-median triangulation could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum density thresholds (only 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage peer identified). Public-company P/S anchoring, though 3 public biotech comps show a median 7.8x multiple, cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue for the target.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $55M to $75M, with $65M as the median point. This range reflects ±15% variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular peer financials at seed stage and lack of target revenue disclosure, which limits triangulation breadth. A subsequent financing round, product-stage milestones, or disclosed financial metrics would materially improve valuation precision.
